title: quercus coahuilensis
intro: Quercus coahuilensis, also known as the Coahuila oak, is a species of oak tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The bark and leaves of Quercus coahuilensis have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus coahuilensis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparation of Quercus coahuilensis typically involves drying and infusing the bark and leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used to promote overall well-being.
